speaker: CALCHAS
text: Now, princes, for the service I have done you,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Th’ advantage of the time prompts me aloud

speaker: CALCHAS
text: To call for recompense. Appear it to your mind

speaker: CALCHAS
text: That, through the sight I bear in things to come,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: I have abandoned Troy, left my possessions,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Incurred a traitor’s name, exposed myself,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: From certain and possessed conveniences,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: To doubtful fortunes, sequest’ring from me all

speaker: CALCHAS
text: That time, acquaintance, custom, and condition

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Made tame and most familiar to my nature,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: And here, to do you service, am become

speaker: CALCHAS
text: As new into the world, strange, unacquainted.

speaker: CALCHAS
text: I do beseech you, as in way of taste,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: To give me now a little benefit

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Out of those many regist’red in promise,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Which you say live to come in my behalf.

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: What wouldst thou of us, Trojan, make demand?

speaker: CALCHAS
text: You have a Trojan prisoner called Antenor

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Yesterday took. Troy holds him very dear.

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Oft have you—often have you thanks therefor—

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Desired my Cressid in right great exchange,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Whom Troy hath still denied; but this Antenor,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: I know, is such a wrest in their affairs

speaker: CALCHAS
text: That their negotiations all must slack,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Wanting his manage; and they will almost

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Give us a prince of blood, a son of Priam,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: In change of him. Let him be sent, great princes,

speaker: CALCHAS
text: And he shall buy my daughter; and her presence

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Shall quite strike off all service I have done

speaker: CALCHAS
text: In most accepted pain.

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Let Diomedes bear him,

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: And bring us Cressid hither. Calchas shall have

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: What he requests of us. Good Diomed,

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Furnish you fairly for this interchange.

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Withal, bring word if Hector will tomorrow

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Be answered in his challenge. Ajax is ready.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: This shall I undertake, and ’tis a burden

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Which I am proud to bear.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Achilles stands i’ th’ entrance of his tent.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Please it our General pass strangely by him

speaker: ULYSSES
text: As if he were forgot, and, princes all,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Lay negligent and loose regard upon him.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: I will come last. ’Tis like he’ll question me

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Why such unplausive eyes are bent, why turned on him.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: If so, I have derision medicinable

speaker: ULYSSES
text: To use between your strangeness and his pride,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Which his own will shall have desire to drink.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: It may do good; pride hath no other glass

speaker: ULYSSES
text: To show itself but pride, for supple knees

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Feed arrogance and are the proud man’s fees.

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: We’ll execute your purpose and put on

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: A form of strangeness as we pass along;

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: So do each lord, and either greet him not

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Or else disdainfully, which shall shake him more

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Than if not looked on. I will lead the way.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: What, comes the General to speak with me?

speaker: ACHILLES
text: You know my mind: I’ll fight no more ’gainst Troy.

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: What says Achilles? Would he aught with us?

speaker: NESTOR
text: Would you, my lord, aught with the General?

speaker: ACHILLES
text: No.

speaker: NESTOR
text: Nothing, my lord.

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: The better.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Good day, good day.

speaker: MENELAUS
text: How do you? How do you?

speaker: ACHILLES
text: What, does the cuckold scorn me?

speaker: AJAX
text: How now, Patroclus?

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Good morrow, Ajax.

speaker: AJAX
text: Ha?

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Good morrow.

speaker: AJAX
text: Ay, and good next day too.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: What mean these fellows? Know they not Achilles?

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: They pass by strangely. They were used to bend,

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: To send their smiles before them to Achilles,

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: To come as humbly as they use to creep

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: To holy altars.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: What, am I poor of late?

speaker: ACHILLES
text: ’Tis certain, greatness, once fall’n out with Fortune,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Must fall out with men too. What the declined is

speaker: ACHILLES
text: He shall as soon read in the eyes of others

speaker: ACHILLES
text: As feel in his own fall, for men, like butterflies,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Show not their mealy wings but to the summer,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: And not a man, for being simply man,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Hath any honor, but honor for those honors

speaker: ACHILLES
text: That are without him—as place, riches, and favor,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Prizes of accident as oft as merit,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Which, when they fall, as being slippery slanders,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: The love that leaned on them, as slippery too,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Doth one pluck down another and together

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Die in the fall. But ’tis not so with me.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Fortune and I are friends. I do enjoy,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: At ample point, all that I did possess,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Save these men’s looks, who do, methinks, find out

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Something not worth in me such rich beholding

speaker: ACHILLES
text: As they have often given. Here is Ulysses.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: I’ll interrupt his reading.—How now, Ulysses?

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Now, great Thetis’ son—

speaker: ACHILLES
text: What are you reading?

speaker: ULYSSES
text: A strange fellow here

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Writes me that man, how dearly ever parted,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: How much in having, or without or in,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Cannot make boast to have that which he hath,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Nor feels not what he owes, but by reflection;

speaker: ULYSSES
text: As when his virtues, shining upon others,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Heat them, and they retort that heat again

speaker: ULYSSES
text: To the first giver.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: This is not strange, Ulysses.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: The beauty that is borne here in the face

speaker: ACHILLES
text: The bearer knows not, but commends itself

speaker: ACHILLES
text: To others’ eyes; nor doth the eye itself,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: That most pure spirit of sense, behold itself,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Not going from itself, but eye to eye opposed

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Salutes each other with each other’s form.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: For speculation turns not to itself

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Till it hath traveled and is mirrored there

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Where it may see itself. This is not strange at all.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: I do not strain at the position—

speaker: ULYSSES
text: It is familiar—but at the author’s drift,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Who in his circumstance expressly proves

speaker: ULYSSES
text: That no man is the lord of anything—

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Though in and of him there be much consisting—

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Till he communicate his parts to others;

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Nor doth he of himself know them for aught

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Till he behold them formed in the applause

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Where they’re extended; who, like an arch, reverb’rate

speaker: ULYSSES
text: The voice again or, like a gate of steel

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Fronting the sun, receives and renders back

speaker: ULYSSES
text: His figure and his heat. I was much rapt in this

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And apprehended here immediately

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Th’ unknown Ajax. Heavens, what a man is there!

speaker: ULYSSES
text: A very horse, that has he knows not what!

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Nature, what things there are

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Most abject in regard, and dear in use,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: What things again most dear in the esteem

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And poor in worth! Now shall we see tomorrow—

speaker: ULYSSES
text: An act that very chance doth throw upon him—

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Ajax renowned. O, heavens, what some men do

speaker: ULYSSES
text: While some men leave to do!

speaker: ULYSSES
text: How some men creep in skittish Fortune’s hall,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Whiles others play the idiots in her eyes!

speaker: ULYSSES
text: How one man eats into another’s pride,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: While pride is fasting in his wantonness!

speaker: ULYSSES
text: To see these Grecian lords—why, even already

speaker: ULYSSES
text: They clap the lubber Ajax on the shoulder

speaker: ULYSSES
text: As if his foot were on brave Hector’s breast

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And great Troy shrieking.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: I do believe it, for they passed by me

speaker: ACHILLES
text: As misers do by beggars, neither gave to me

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Good word nor look. What, are my deeds forgot?

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Time hath, my lord, a wallet at his back

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Wherein he puts alms for oblivion,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: A great-sized monster of ingratitudes.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Those scraps are good deeds past, which are devoured

speaker: ULYSSES
text: As fast as they are made, forgot as soon

speaker: ULYSSES
text: As done. Perseverance, dear my lord,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Keeps honor bright. To have done is to hang

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Quite out of fashion like a rusty mail

speaker: ULYSSES
text: In monumental mock’ry. Take the instant way,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: For honor travels in a strait so narrow

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Where one but goes abreast. Keep, then, the path,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: For Emulation hath a thousand sons

speaker: ULYSSES
text: That one by one pursue. If you give way

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Or turn aside from the direct forthright,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Like to an entered tide they all rush by

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And leave you hindmost;

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Or, like a gallant horse fall’n in first rank,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Lie there for pavement to the abject rear,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: O’errun and trampled on. Then what they do in present,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Though less than yours in past, must o’ertop yours;

speaker: ULYSSES
text: For Time is like a fashionable host

speaker: ULYSSES
text: That slightly shakes his parting guest by th’ hand

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And, with his arms outstretched as he would fly,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Grasps in the comer. Welcome ever smiles,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And Farewell goes out sighing. Let not virtue seek

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Remuneration for the thing it was,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: For beauty, wit,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: High birth, vigor of bone, desert in service,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Love, friendship, charity are subjects all

speaker: ULYSSES
text: To envious and calumniating Time.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: One touch of nature makes the whole world kin,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: That all, with one consent, praise newborn gauds,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Though they are made and molded of things past,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And give to dust that is a little gilt

speaker: ULYSSES
text: More laud than gilt o’erdusted.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: The present eye praises the present object.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Then marvel not, thou great and complete man,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: That all the Greeks begin to worship Ajax,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Since things in motion sooner catch the eye

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Than what stirs not. The cry went once on thee,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And still it might, and yet it may again,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: If thou wouldst not entomb thyself alive

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And case thy reputation in thy tent,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Whose glorious deeds but in these fields of late

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Made emulous missions ’mongst the gods themselves

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And drave great Mars to faction.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Of this my privacy,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: I have strong reasons.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: But ’gainst your privacy

speaker: ULYSSES
text: The reasons are more potent and heroical.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: ’Tis known, Achilles, that you are in love

speaker: ULYSSES
text: With one of Priam’s daughters.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Ha? Known?

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Is that a wonder?

speaker: ULYSSES
text: The providence that’s in a watchful state

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Knows almost every grain of Pluto’s gold,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Finds bottom in the uncomprehensive deep,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Keeps place with thought and almost, like the gods,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Do thoughts unveil in their dumb cradles.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: There is a mystery—with whom relation

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Durst never meddle—in the soul of state,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Which hath an operation more divine

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Than breath or pen can give expressure to.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: All the commerce that you have had with Troy

speaker: ULYSSES
text: As perfectly is ours as yours, my lord;

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And better would it fit Achilles much

speaker: ULYSSES
text: To throw down Hector than Polyxena.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: But it must grieve young Pyrrhus now at home

speaker: ULYSSES
text: When Fame shall in our islands sound her trump,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And all the Greekish girls shall tripping sing

speaker: ULYSSES
text: “Great Hector’s sister did Achilles win,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: But our great Ajax bravely beat down him.”

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Farewell, my lord. I as your lover speak.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: The fool slides o’er the ice that you should break.

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: To this effect, Achilles, have I moved you.

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: A woman impudent and mannish grown

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Is not more loathed than an effeminate man

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: In time of action. I stand condemned for this.

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: They think my little stomach to the war,

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: And your great love to me, restrains you thus.

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Sweet, rouse yourself, and the weak wanton Cupid

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Shall from your neck unloose his amorous fold

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: And, like a dewdrop from the lion’s mane,

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Be shook to air.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Shall Ajax fight with Hector?

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Ay, and perhaps receive much honor by him.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: I see my reputation is at stake;

speaker: ACHILLES
text: My fame is shrewdly gored.

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: O, then, beware!

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Those wounds heal ill that men do give themselves.

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Omission to do what is necessary

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Seals a commission to a blank of danger,

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: And danger, like an ague, subtly taints

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Even then when they sit idly in the sun.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Go call Thersites hither, sweet Patroclus.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: I’ll send the fool to Ajax and desire him

speaker: ACHILLES
text: T’ invite the Trojan lords after the combat

speaker: ACHILLES
text: To see us here unarmed. I have a woman’s longing,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: An appetite that I am sick withal,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: To see great Hector in his weeds of peace,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: To talk with him, and to behold his visage,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Even to my full of view.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: A labor saved.

speaker: THERSITES
text: A wonder!

speaker: ACHILLES
text: What?

speaker: THERSITES
text: Ajax goes up and down the field, asking for

speaker: THERSITES
text: himself.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: How so?

speaker: THERSITES
text: He must fight singly tomorrow with Hector

speaker: THERSITES
text: and is so prophetically proud of an heroical cudgeling

speaker: THERSITES
text: that he raves in saying nothing.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: How can that be?

speaker: THERSITES
text: Why, he stalks up and down like a peacock—

speaker: THERSITES
text: a stride and a stand; ruminates like an hostess

speaker: THERSITES
text: that hath no arithmetic but her brain to set

speaker: THERSITES
text: down her reckoning; bites his lip with a politic regard,

speaker: THERSITES
text: as who should say “There were wit in this

speaker: THERSITES
text: head an ’twould out”—and so there is, but it lies

speaker: THERSITES
text: as coldly in him as fire in a flint, which will not

speaker: THERSITES
text: show without knocking. The man’s undone forever,

speaker: THERSITES
text: for if Hector break not his neck i’ th’ combat,

speaker: THERSITES
text: he’ll break ’t himself in vainglory. He knows not

speaker: THERSITES
text: me. I said “Good morrow, Ajax,” and he replies

speaker: THERSITES
text: “Thanks, Agamemnon.” What think you of this

speaker: THERSITES
text: man that takes me for the General? He’s grown a

speaker: THERSITES
text: very land-fish, languageless, a monster. A plague of

speaker: THERSITES
text: opinion! A man may wear it on both sides, like a

speaker: THERSITES
text: leather jerkin.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Thou must be my ambassador to him,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Thersites.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Who, I? Why, he’ll answer nobody. He professes

speaker: THERSITES
text: not answering; speaking is for beggars; he

speaker: THERSITES
text: wears his tongue in ’s arms. I will put on his presence.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Let Patroclus make his demands to me. You

speaker: THERSITES
text: shall see the pageant of Ajax.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: To him, Patroclus. Tell him I humbly desire

speaker: ACHILLES
text: the valiant Ajax to invite the most valorous Hector

speaker: ACHILLES
text: to come unarmed to my tent, and to procure safe-conduct

speaker: ACHILLES
text: for his person of the magnanimous and

speaker: ACHILLES
text: most illustrious, six-or-seven-times-honored captain

speaker: ACHILLES
text: general of the Grecian army, Agamemnon,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: et cetera. Do this.

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Jove

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: bless great Ajax.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Hum!

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: I come from the worthy Achilles—

speaker: THERSITES
text: Ha?

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Who most humbly desires you to invite

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Hector to his tent—

speaker: THERSITES
text: Hum!

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: And to procure safe-conduct from

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Agamemnon.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Agamemnon?

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Ay, my lord.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Ha!

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: What say you to ’t?

speaker: THERSITES
text: God b’ wi’ you, with all my heart.

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Your answer, sir.

speaker: THERSITES
text: If tomorrow be a fair day, by eleven of the

speaker: THERSITES
text: clock it will go one way or other. Howsoever, he

speaker: THERSITES
text: shall pay for me ere he has me.

speaker: PATROCLUS
text: Your answer, sir.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Fare you well with all my heart.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Why, but he is not in this tune, is he?

speaker: THERSITES
text: No, but he’s out of tune thus. What music

speaker: THERSITES
text: will be in him when Hector has knocked out his

speaker: THERSITES
text: brains I know not. But I am sure none, unless the

speaker: THERSITES
text: fiddler Apollo get his sinews to make catlings on.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Come, thou shalt bear a letter to him

speaker: ACHILLES
text: straight.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Let me bear another to his horse, for that’s

speaker: THERSITES
text: the more capable creature.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: My mind is troubled, like a fountain stirred,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: And I myself see not the bottom of it.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Would the fountain of your mind were clear

speaker: THERSITES
text: again, that I might water an ass at it. I had rather

speaker: THERSITES
text: be a tick in a sheep than such a valiant ignorance.
